channel.title: Dr. Brittany E. Miles
channel.description: Recent content on Dr. Brittany E. Miles

item.title: Course Title 2
item.description: This undergraduate course presents many new things.

Research Opportunities I am looking for graduate students and postdocs to join my research group. If you are interested in collaborating or pursuing research within my research group email me at bemiles@arizona.edu .

About I completed my bachelor’s in Physics and minor in Geophysics and Planetary Physics at UCLA. After undergrad I moved to UC Santa Cruz and earned my master’s and doctorate in Astronomy and Astrophysics. During my time at Santa Cruz, I was a Eugene Cota-Robles and NSF Graduate Research Fellow. Post-PhD, I spent a summer working at UC Irvine as a UC presidential postdoctoral fellow. I finally moved to the University of Arizona as a 51 Pegasi b and Presidential Fellow, then transistioned into my current role as an assistant professor.
IFU Upgrade for MIRAC-5 on the MMT
Thermal-IR Exoplanet Imager and Spectrograph for the Large Binocular Telescope

ASTR302: Introduction to Observational Astronomy Course Description Students will learn how to develop and execute an observing program with optical/infrared telescopes. Experience with computers and some python is required. We will cover how various types of telescopes and their instruments work to take data across the electromagnetic spectrum in various formats. Students will learn about measurement error and error propagation along with its application in interpreting astronomical observations. Weather permitting, students will execute their own observing program in small groups using the 61” Kuiper Telescope.

item.title: A 1 to 20 μm Spectrum of VHS 1256–1257 b
item.description: Uncovering theturbulent and cloudy atmosphere of VHS 1256b with JWST.

item.title: Disequilibrium CO Chemistry in the Coldest Brown Dwarfs
item.description: Bridging the gap between chemistry in the coldest brown dwarfs and Solar System gas giant planets.

item.title: Location
item.description: Mailing and office addresses at the University of Place.

item.title: Long Wavelength Detectors and Spectroscopy
item.description: Always looking for the perfect detector that doesnt exist yet.

item.title: Probing the Depths
item.description: Mapping 1-D Mixing Profiles of Brown Dwarfs with JWST.
